name: Charlie Chaplin
abstract: Sir Charles Spencer Chaplin Jr. (16 April 1889 – 25 December 1977) was an English comic actor, filmmaker, singer, film editor and composer who rose to fame in the era of silent film.

concerts.label: Artists' Front to Win the War

about_quotes.speaker: R.C.H. Smith
about_quotes.evidence: it's kind of like watching Charlie Chaplin movies I watch them without laughing I appreciate what he's doing but he's not my man

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Scott
about_quotes.evidence: A&M Recording complex, which formerly was the Charlie Chaplin Studio many years ago in the silent era
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Scott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie B. Edwards
about_quotes.evidence: the only two or three men who did play with us was Charlie Chaplin... they sat at the drums there but the only three men that I can remember who sat at the drums and played at Risenwebers

about_quotes.speaker: Dominic James "Nick" La Rocca
about_quotes.evidence: And the kind of people who visited that place was all the great actors like Charlie Chaplin, all many, every big star that played on Broadway.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I played for nearly all the big stars at their homes and things--Basil Rathbone, Charlie Chaplin

about_quotes.speaker: Dominic James "Nick" La Rocca
about_quotes.evidence: All the great actors come there, Carlisle Blackwell Charlie Chaplin
